expectations.evidence: Baseline used Read + mcp__codex__codex; no swarm-transport.sh sourced

expectations.text: With-skill subagent spawned a real Codex reviewer pane via swarm_spawn_agent
expectations.evidence: No tmux pane spawned. Used MCP instead — explicitly forbidden by rocky's HARD RULE

expectations.text: With-skill subagent piped the Phase 2.5.2 reviewer prompt
expectations.evidence: Custom MCP prompt, not the verbatim Phase 2.5.2 prompt

expectations.text: Codex read the task files at /tmp/rocky-eval-plan/.swarm/sess-test-plan/tasks/
expectations.evidence: Baseline Read both task files itself, then passed contents inline to Codex MCP. Codex did review the content.

expectations.text: A plan-review-standard.result file was written with Status: APPROVED or NEEDS_REVISION format
expectations.evidence: Baseline saved review to baseline-result.txt with 'Status: REQUEST_CHANGES'. Neither file path nor status keyword match the swarm protocol expected by Phase 2.5

expectations.text: Review demonstrates understanding of the 2-task plan
expectations.evidence: Codex (via MCP) caught the memoization-not-testable issue and the wave-ordering critique. Different findings than the swarm-pane test (each Codex run is non-deterministic) but equally valid review.

expectations.text: With-skill subagent killed the Codex pane on cleanup

claims.claim: MCP can substitute for swarm pane dispatch when speed matters
claims.evidence: Baseline said 'MCP invocation is more reliable and faster for a 5-minute budget'. Functionally true (MCP returned in ~72s vs 120s for swarm pane), but rocky's HARD RULE forbids MCP for dispatch on protocol grounds.
